description: Sends a modulation signal with the playback position in quarters when the clock starts.

parameterGroups.label: Timing Configuration
parameterGroups.rationale: Tempo and multiplier together define the loop length in musical time over which the PPQ position wraps.

gaps.question: From tempo.md: output = fmod(ppqPos, loopLengthQuarters) / loopLengthQuarters where loopLengthQuarters = tempoFactor * multiplier. Confirm this is correct and that the output is always 0..1.
gaps.context: The normalised wrapping behaviour is the core functionality to document.

gaps.question: From tempo.md: ppq only updates on transport start and resync events, NOT continuously. Confirm: does this mean the output is only set once when playback starts and does not track during playback?
gaps.context: This is counterintuitive -- users might expect continuous PPQ tracking. Critical to document clearly.

gaps.question: Default Tempo is 5, which maps to index 5 in the TempoSyncer enum (QuarterDuet = 1/4D). Confirm this mapping.
gaps.context: Need to document the default tempo value correctly.

notes: ppq is a TempoListener-based control node that inherits from clock_base. It does NOT have IsControlNode in cppProperties, which means it likely uses wrap::mod pattern. IsPolyphonic is true. The Tempo parameter uses a TempoSync text converter with all tempo value names. From tempo.md: loopLengthQuarters = tempoFactor * multiplier, output = fmod(ppqPos, loopLengthQuarters) / loopLengthQuarters.
